description:
* Pantry services are available to anyone who resides in New York City (all 5 boroughs) or Yonkers.

**The FAQs**
* How do I get food?
	* Registration happens on-site.  Bring an ID and proof of address until you receive a client card.  They only allow one person per household (address) to become a client at a time.  If you live with someone who is already a client, they will turn you away.

* Can someone deliver the food to my house?
	* Delivery services are only available for clients who live in Inwood.  The program, Inwood Meals-on-Heels, is very limited and is reserved for clients with disabilities or serious health issues.  If you are an Inwood resident on disability (and have documentation), let them know at foodpantry@cis-nyc.org. 
 
* What do I have to bring?
	* The Cabrini Immigrant Services Food Pantry suggests that you bring a cart.   Bring an ID and proof of address until you receive a client card.  After you receive a client card, bring your client card each time you come.
 
* Do I need an appointment: 
	* No appointment necessary, but clients cannot pick up a food package two weeks in a row.
 
* What kind of food do they have? 
	* The Cabrini Immigrant Services Food Pantry offer grocery-style items such as canned food, fresh produce, frozen meat, and dry goods like pasta or rice that you can cook yourself.
 
* Do they have vegetables? 
	* The Cabrini Immigrant Services Food Pantry gives out 5-8 different types of fruits and vegetables each week.
 
* Do they have meat?
	* The Cabrini Immigrant Services Food Pantry gives out frozen meat each week.
name: Cabrini Immigrant Services Food Pantry in collaboration with Church of the Good Shepherd

description:
* The **Food Stamp Program** provides pre-screening for clients interested in receiving Food Stamps, and recertification for those already enrolled. CCC partners with trained, professional staff from the New York City Coalition Against Hunger and the Food Bank for New York City to offer this service. Please call (212) 316-7583 to set up an appointment. Appointments are available on Tuesdays and Wednesdays.

* If you are interested in volunteering with the Sunday Soup Kitchen, please go to newyorkcares.org and click on “Become a Volunteer." Once you’ve become a New York Cares volunteer, you can select Cathedral Community Cares as your project site.

* Person seeking professional clothing must provide a referral from an agency.

Regular clients must register and must bring ID and proof of residence.

description:
Located in the heart of Greenwich Village, Greenwich House Center on the Square is a vibrant older adult center offering nutritious lunch meals, wellness programs, and a welcoming community for New Yorkers ages 60 and up. Operated by Greenwich House, the Center provides a safe and social environment where members can enjoy hot lunches, connect with peers, and access support that promotes healthy aging.

In addition to week day congregate lunch meals, Center on the Square offers health and fitness classes, arts and cultural programming, social services, and opportunities to stay active and engaged. Membership is free for adults 60+, and meals are available on a suggested contribution basis—no one is turned away for inability to pay.

Center on the Square is open Monday–Friday and is an important neighborhood resource for older adults seeking wellness, creativity, and connection.
name: Greenwich House Center on the Square

description:
How to Apply
You must submit a SNAP application form and required documents to apply or recertify for SNAP benefits. You can use any of the following formats to apply and submit required documents: 
Online 
The quickest way to apply or recertify for SNAP benefits is online. You can use the ACCESS HRA website or the ACCESS HRA Mobile App to apply or recertify for SNAP benefits. You can submit documents via the “Document Upload” section of the mobile app. 
Download a SNAP application from HRA's website:
SNAP Benefits Application (LDSS-4826):http://otda.state.nyenet/ldss_eforms/eforms/4826A.pdf
In Person 
You can pick up and submit an application from any SNAP Walk-in Customer Service Center. Find a SNAP Walk-in Customer Service Center near you.
You can also visit a local Community Based Organization (CBO) for application assistance, eligibility screening, and to submit documents. Find a CBO near you. 
By Phone (DSS OneNumber) Call (718) 557-1399 Business Hours: Monday - Friday: 8 AM - 5 PM 
You can reach staff during business hours (except for City Holidays). You can get help in most languages. Automated Assistance is available 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
To Mail Completed Applications: 
Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program 
O. Box 29008 
Brooklyn, NY 11202 
To Fax Completed Applications/ Documents: 
Fax your application form along with required documents to:
917-639-1111 – New application 
917-639-2544 – Recertification
If you need to submit documents after you already submitted your application form, fax the documents to 917-639-2544.
name: Manhattan Food Stamp Center

*  **Choice Pantry Program** 
	* Offers fresh, culturally appropriate and nutritious food free to participants who qualify.
	* Pantry packages are supplemental grocery packages in five food categories: protein, dairy, grains, fruits and vegetables, proportional to the number of individuals in the family and provided every two weeks.
	* Please bring photo ID for all adults in the household, ID for all children in the household, e.g., birth certificate, school letter, and proof of address, e.g., rent receipt or lease.
	
* **Hot Meals/Brown Bag Lunches Program** provides hot, nutritionally balanced meals to our guests during weekdays and a sandwich, snack and beverage for take-away during weekends.  Everyone is welcome.



Requires ID for adults and birth certificates for children in the household

Will be given a Pantry ID upon registration

description_es:
- El programa Kosher Meals at Home entrega comidas a adultos mayores que mantienen la comida kosher (o Halal) y viven en el **Upper West Side** (de 50th Street a 125th Street) y el **Upper East Side** (de 50th Street). Calle hasta la calle 96).

- Las comidas están congeladas y vienen en una bandeja estilo “TV Dinner” con una proteína, un almidón y una verdura. Cada semana se entregan de tres a cinco comidas (a los adultos mayores se les asigna un día de entrega y un período de tiempo para esperar la entrega). Hay una contribución de escala móvil de **$5 por comida** hasta **$1 por comida**.

- Puede haber una lista de espera para este programa (cambia semana a semana), pero en una situación de emergencia, Kosher Meals at Home generalmente puede ayudar con las comidas a corto plazo hasta que una agencia de administración de casos pueda hacerse cargo de la entrega de las comidas.

- El adulto mayor que solicita comidas debe poder calentarlas de manera segura en un microondas o en un horno normal por sí solo (o tener un asistente o familiar/cuidador que pueda calentarlas). Las comidas no se pueden calentar en un horno tostador a menos que se transfieran a otro plato que sea seguro.
description:
- The Kosher Meals at Home program delivers meals to senior adults who keep kosher (or Halal) and live on the **Upper West Side** (from 50th Street to 125th Street) and the **Upper East Side** (from 50th Street to 96th Street). 

- Meals are frozen and come in a “TV Dinner” style tray with a protein, a starch, and a vegetable. Three to five meals are delivered each week (older adults are assigned a delivery day and window of time to expect the delivery). There is a sliding-scale contribution of **$5/meal** down to **$1/meal**.

- There may be a wait list for this program (it changes week-to-week), but in an emergency situation, Kosher Meals at Home can usually assist with meals short-term until a case management agency can take over the meal delivery. 

- The older adult requesting meals needs to be able to safely heat the meals in a microwave or regular oven on their own (or have an aide or family member/caregiver who can heat the meals for them). Meals cannot be heated in a toaster oven unless they are transferred to another dish that is safe.
name: Project DOROT

description_es:
Sus asesores lo ayudarán a averiguar si califica para SNAP, recopilar los documentos requeridos y enviar su solicitud. También le proporcionarán referencias a otros programas que pueda necesitar, como WIC y seguro médico.

Llame para comenzar con la inscripción a SNAP por teléfono. Es posible que deba dejar un mensaje de voz, pero alguien le devolverá la llamada dentro de las 48 horas. Encuentre el número de su distrito a continuación:
* Bronx y Manhattan: 646-584-1553
* Brooklyn: 646-819-2451 
* Para español: Jose 646-793-0841/ Rosemary 646-399-4822 
* (Para los servicios SNAP del Kings County Hospital, llame al 646-771-5620)
* Queens: 646-942-2431
* Staten Island: 929-270-2181

También puede completar su formulario de solicitud en línea y alguien se comunicará con usted: https://www.healthsolutions.org/find-services/
description:
Their counselors will help you find out if you qualify for SNAP, gather required documents, and submit your application. They will also provide referrals to other programs you may need, like WIC and health insurance.

Call to get started with SNAP enrollment over the phone. You may have to leave a voicemail, but someone will call you back within 48 hours. Find the number for your borough below:
* Bronx and Manhattan: 646-584-1553
* Brooklyn: 646-819-2451 For Spanish: Jose 646-793-0841/ Rosemary 646-399-4822 (For Kings County Hospital SNAP services, call 646-771-5620)
* Queens: 646-942-2431
* Staten Island: 929-270-2181

You can also fill out their request form online, and someone will get back to you: https://www.healthsolutions.org/find-services/

description:
Each week, TUESDAY, we host a choice pantry with fresh vegetables, fruits and curated ingredients from our network of supporters. Each guest receives at least 14 pounds of produce plus additional staples such as bread, grains, eggs. We believe in a dignified model in which guests select which items they will enjoy.  Each week we provide food education information, health and cooking tips. 

On Wednesday & Friday evenings our social kitchen prepares an evening meal for guests for free.  We seat 100 guests at communal tables in the main community hall.  We offer a multi-course meal, appetizer, entree and dessert, served with NA beverages.  We offer a social experience with  full service at the table, to give guests an opportunity to relax, enjoy the company of others and a delicious meal.

description:
They are currently serving Manhattan, Brooklyn, Queens, the Bronx, and Long Island.
Complete a brief online form, and a Rethink Food team member will follow up with next steps.
You may be eligible if you:
- Use emergency or hospital services often
- Are in a NYS Health Home with conditions like HIV, serious mental illness, or multiple chronic conditions
- Have a substance use disorder or developmental disability
- Are pregnant or recently postpartum
- Were recently released from incarceration with a chronic condition
- Are a high-risk child or youth, including those in foster or kinship care

Medicaid members are encouraged to apply to assess eligibility.

An ID is not required but highly recommended for ease of correctly transcribing community members' names and addresses for TEFAP requirements. 

We currently have a waitlist for appointments but folks are also welcome to email us at pantry@girlsclub.org to add their information to our waitlist.

description_es:
Nos hemos asociado con Plentiful para ofrecer citas a nuestros huéspedes para la despensa. Agendar una cita:
Dos opciones:
Descarga la aplicación aquí. Disponible para Android en la tienda Google Play. (No disponible para iOS)
A través de mensaje de texto
Envíe un mensaje de texto con la palabra "SAFH" al 726-879 (DESPENSA)
Espere a que el sistema le responda el mensaje de texto; esto puede tardar unos minutos.
Responda las preguntas que le haga el sistema (por ejemplo, nombre, fecha de nacimiento, código postal, número de personas en el hogar, etc.)
Seleccione la fecha y hora de su cita preferida
• Si por primera vez no tienes nada, no hay problema. Aún así se le servirá, pero solo recibirá comida para una persona y no para toda su familia. La próxima vez deberás traer documentación contigo.
description:
We have partnered with Plentiful to provide appointments to our guests for the pantry. To schedule an appointment:
Two options:
Download the app here. Available for Android on the Google Play store. (Not available for iOS)
Via Text Message
Text the word "SAFH" to 726-879 (PANTRY)
Wait for the system to text you back - this may take a few minutes.
Respond to the questions that the system asks (e.g. Name, Date of Birth, Zip Code, Number of People in the Household, etc.)
Select your preferred appointment date and time
• If for your first time, you don't have anything, that is no problem. You will still be served, but only receive food for one person, and not your entire household. You will need to bring documentation with you next time.

Requires ID for adults and birth certificates for children in the household
name: Trinity’s Services & Food for the Homeless Inc
